# detect_ghost { #tspaint.detect_ghost }

```python
tspaint.detect_ghost(
    ts,
    labels,
    samples=None,
    *,
    depth='time',
    max_iter=50,
    tol=0.001,
    delta=None,
    init_archaic_burden=0.1,
    init_switch=0.02,
    n_jobs=None,
    progress=False,
)
```

Reference-free ghost / archaic introgression detection via a depth-emission HMM.

The dedicated ghost-search tool (Task 2; CLAUDE.md §9). A 2-state (modern / ghost) HMM along the
genome on nearest-modern-reference coalescence depth — the modern emission anchored by the
panel, the **ghost** emission learned and constrained deeper. Returns a **calibrated** per-locus
``P(ghost)`` with no ghost/archaic reference required. The signal is depth, so it targets **deep
(archaic-like)** ghosts. (Renamed from ``detect_archaic``, which remains a deprecated alias.)

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**ts**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`) or [list](`list`)\[[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   One tree sequence, or **an ensemble** (e.g. SINGER posterior samples) — the HMM is fit once pooled across all members, decoded per member, and the per-locus ``P(ghost)`` **averaged** across members (marginalising ARG uncertainty for accuracy; CLAUDE.md §7.4). All members must share the same sample ids.

<code>[**labels**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [int](`int`)\] or [iterable](`iterable`)\[[int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The modern reference sample ids (a ``{ref: state}`` painting label dict is accepted — only the ids are used; every reference is "modern"). They anchor the modern depth distribution.

<code>[**samples**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[iterable](`iterable`)\[[int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Samples to scan; defaults to every non-reference sample.

<code>[**depth**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [(\'time\', \'rank\')]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [\"time\"]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   The depth observation: ``"time"`` (default) uses ``log`` nearest-reference coalescence time; ``"rank"`` uses its genome-wide span-weighted **rank** — a monotonic transform, hence **calibration-invariant** (robust to branch-length miscalibration, e.g. on a Relate ARG).

<code>[**max_iter**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [([int](`int`), [float](`float`))]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [50]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Baum–Welch iteration cap and log-likelihood tolerance.

<code>[**tol**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [([int](`int`), [float](`float`))]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [50]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Baum–Welch iteration cap and log-likelihood tolerance.

<code>[**delta**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Minimum gap ``μ_ghost - μ_modern`` enforced above the panel's deepest coalescence (in the ``depth`` units). Defaults to ``σ_modern``.

<code>[**init_archaic_burden**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.1]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Initial ghost fraction and per-breakpoint switch probability.

<code>[**init_switch**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.1]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Initial ghost fraction and per-breakpoint switch probability.

<code>[**n_jobs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Worker processes for the nearest-modern-ref coalescence pass (split by genome chunk, one shared pool across ensemble members; exactly equal to serial). Default: all CPUs / the SLURM allocation (:func:`tspaint.parallel.resolve_cores`); pass ``1`` for serial.

<code>[**progress**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [False]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Show a progress bar for the depth pass. Default ``False``.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[GhostResult](`tspaint.archaic.GhostResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per-sample ``P(ghost)`` posteriors / tracts / burden plus the learned HMM parameters. For an ensemble the posteriors are the per-member mean.

## Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

<code>[:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[ValueError](`ValueError`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   If the modern anchor cannot be estimated (no informative reference depths), ``depth`` is invalid, or the ensemble is empty.
